note_on_metrics: Token/duration telemetry is not exposed to run subagents; token values are word_count * 1.3 approximations and should be treated as rough, not authoritative.
provenance: Trigger phase reconstructed from the verified u9i.3.5 sweep (21/21) against the unchanged committed description. Qualitative eval-22/eval-23 from the original sweep; eval-24 baseline recovered via a single state-independent no-skill re-run (original froze under the watchdog).

trigger_accuracy.bar: PASS (0 FP on near-miss negatives; >=90% recall)

per_eval.eval: 22 — coding agent forgetting decisions across multi-file refactor
per_eval.verdict: with-skill better: volatile-data-last (status bar at END), sub-agent isolation for file reads, process-driven prompt; baseline puts decision log at prompt START (busts cache). CONDITIONAL PASS (longer, substantive).

per_eval.eval: 23 — customer support agent, inconsistent domain handling
per_eval.verdict: with-skill better: audit -> process restructure -> domain isolation -> volatile-last cache placement; baseline good intuition but no framework, no cache-awareness. CONDITIONAL PASS (longer, substantive).

per_eval.eval: 24 — autonomous research agent, design from scratch
per_eval.verdict: with-skill better AND shorter: leads with isolation-over-compression, names long-run failure modes with explicit mitigations. PASS (strict invisibility).

qualitative.invisibility_verdict: PASS on substance-not-ceremony across all three; eval-24 passes the strict same-or-fewer-tokens test outright.

overall: SHIP — 100% trigger accuracy (0 FP), qualitative lift on all three prompts. Extra tokens (where present) are substantive domain guidance, not ceremony.
